title: potentilla clevelandii var. brevibracteata
intro: Potentilla clevelandii var. brevibracteata, also known as Cleveland's cinquefoil, is a perennial herb that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Potentilla clevelandii var. brevibracteata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
